title: A recipe written on the back of a map
summary: Tell the story of an old recipe found in an unexpected place, and what it says about the person who wrote it.
topic: An old recipe found in an unexpected place
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences telling the story of an old recipe or note you found in a strange place, such as inside a book, on the back of a map, or in an old coat. Describe what it said and who you think wrote it. Use past simple and the connectors 'when', 'then', and 'so'.
hintStarters: I found it by accident when I was looking for…
On the back, in faded pencil, someone had written…
At first I did not understand why a recipe was…
sampleText: Last winter I cleared out my grandfather's desk in Tbilisi, and in the bottom drawer I found an old folded map of the Black Sea coast. The map itself was not interesting; the roads were wrong now and the ink was brown. But when I turned it over, I saw faded pencil writing in the corner. It was a recipe for a fish stew. Someone had written the ingredients in two languages, Georgian and Russian, and next to each one there was a small number, maybe the price at the time. The handwriting was small and careful, and it was not my grandfather's. I asked my mother, and she said it was my great-grandmother's hand. She used to cook on the boats that crossed the sea, so she wrote her recipes on whatever paper she had. The map was probably the only clean paper near her that day. I read the recipe slowly. It said to add the spices last and to taste before adding salt, because the fish was already salty. Then I understood why she kept it on a map: she was always travelling, and the recipe travelled with her. I could not read every word, so some of it stays a small mystery. I keep the map in a frame now, recipe side out, so people ask about it.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: dọn sạch bàn làm việc của ông tôi
tấm bản đồ cũ gấp lại

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a recipe for a fish stew
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: công thức nấu món cá hầm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the handwriting was small and careful
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nét chữ nhỏ và cẩn thận
đó là nét chữ của cụ tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she used to cook on the boats
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cụ từng nấu ăn trên những con thuyền
bất cứ tờ giấy nào cụ có
cho gia vị vào sau cùng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: some of it stays a small mystery
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một phần của nó vẫn là điều bí ẩn nhỏ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I keep the map in a frame now
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: giờ tôi giữ tấm bản đồ trong khung

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'When' and 'then' to order events in a story
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'when I turned it over, I saw faded pencil writing' dùng 'when' để nối hai hành động xảy ra cùng lúc trong quá khứ. Sau đó 'Then I understood why she kept it on a map' dùng 'then' để chỉ điều xảy ra tiếp theo. Cả hai giúp sắp xếp thứ tự các sự kiện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'when I turned it over, I saw faded pencil writing' uses 'when' to link two past actions that happen together. Later, 'Then I understood why she kept it on a map' uses 'then' to show what happened next. Both help put events in order.

sampleTranslationVi: Mùa đông năm ngoái tôi dọn dẹp bàn làm việc của ông ở Tbilisi, và trong ngăn kéo dưới cùng tôi tìm thấy một tấm bản đồ cũ gấp lại của bờ Biển Đen. Bản thân tấm bản đồ không thú vị; các con đường giờ đã sai và mực đã ngả nâu. Nhưng khi tôi lật mặt sau, tôi thấy nét bút chì đã phai ở góc. Đó là công thức nấu món cá hầm. Ai đó đã viết nguyên liệu bằng hai thứ tiếng, tiếng Georgia và tiếng Nga, và bên cạnh mỗi thứ có một con số nhỏ, có lẽ là giá lúc đó. Nét chữ nhỏ và cẩn thận, và đó không phải nét chữ của ông tôi. Tôi hỏi mẹ, và bà nói đó là nét chữ của cụ tôi. Cụ từng nấu ăn trên những con thuyền vượt biển, nên cụ viết công thức lên bất cứ tờ giấy nào cụ có. Tấm bản đồ có lẽ là tờ giấy sạch duy nhất gần cụ ngày hôm đó. Tôi đọc công thức chầm chậm. Nó nói hãy cho gia vị vào sau cùng và nếm trước khi cho muối, vì cá đã mặn sẵn rồi. Rồi tôi hiểu vì sao cụ giữ nó trên một tấm bản đồ: cụ luôn đi lại, và công thức đi theo cụ. Tôi không đọc được hết từng chữ, nên một phần của nó vẫn là điều bí ẩn nhỏ. Giờ tôi giữ tấm bản đồ trong khung, để mặt công thức ra ngoài, để mọi người hỏi về nó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence about finding the map.
options: In the bottom drawer I found an old folded map.
In the bottom drawer I found an old folding map.
In the bottom drawer I find an old folded map.
In the bottom drawer I found a old folded map.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Old folded map' uses the correct past participle as an adjective. 'Folding map' changes the meaning; 'I find' is present, not past; 'a old' should be 'an old'.
mcQuestions.hint: Is the map being folded by us now, or already folded?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about turning the map over.
options: When I turned over it, I saw faded pencil writing.
When I turned it over, I saw faded pencil writing.
When I turned it over, I saw faded pencil writting.
When I turned it over, I have seen faded pencil writing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Turned it over' correctly places the pronoun before 'over'. 'Turned over it' is wrong; 'writting' is misspelled; the present perfect 'have seen' clashes with the past 'turned'.
mcQuestions.hint: With a pronoun object, does it go before or after 'over'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence using 'so' to explain a result.
options: She had no paper, because she wrote on the map.
She had no paper, so she wrote in the map.
She had no paper, so she wrote on the map.
She had any paper, so she wrote on the map.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'So' shows the result correctly with 'on the map'. 'Because' shows a reason; 'in the map' is the wrong preposition; 'had any paper' should be 'had no paper' in a positive statement.
mcQuestions.hint: Do you write 'on' or 'in' a piece of paper?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the best sentence about the recipe in two languages.
options: Someone had wrote the ingredients in two languages.
Someone had written the ingredients on two languages.
Someone has written the ingredients in two languages then.
Someone had written the ingredients in two languages.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had written' is the correct past perfect. 'Had wrote' uses the wrong participle; 'on two languages' uses the wrong preposition; 'has written … then' mixes present perfect with a past marker.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past participle of 'write'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about the great-grandmother's past job.
options: She used to cook on the boats that crossed the sea.
She use to cook on the boats that crossed the sea.
She used to cooking on the boats that crossed the sea.
She was used to cook on the boats that crossed the sea.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Used to cook' is the correct past-habit form. 'Use to' drops the 'd'; 'used to cooking' uses the wrong form; 'was used to cook' means a different thing.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'used to', do we use the base verb or the -ing form?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best sentence giving the cooking instruction.
options: It said to add the spices last and to taste before to add salt.
It said to add the spices last and to taste before adding salt.
It said add the spices last and taste before adding the salt then.
It said to add the spices lastly and to taste before adding salt.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Before adding salt' correctly uses the -ing form after 'before'. Option 2 uses 'to add'; option 3 omits 'to' after 'said' and adds 'then'; 'lastly' is unnatural for order of steps here.
mcQuestions.hint: After the preposition 'before', do we use 'to add' or 'adding'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that best uses 'then' to show the next step in thinking.
options: Then I understood why did she keep it on a map.
Then I understood why she keeped it on a map.
Then I understood why she kept it on a map.
Then I understanded why she kept it on a map.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Why she kept it' uses correct statement word order and past tense. Option 2 keeps question order; 'keeped' and 'understanded' are wrong past forms.
mcQuestions.hint: In an indirect question after 'understood', does the word order invert?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the most natural sentence about the recipe going with her.
options: The recipe travelled by her.
The recipe was travelled with her.
The recipe travelling with her.
The recipe travelled with her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Travelled with her' is correct. 'By her' changes the meaning; 'was travelled' is a wrong passive; 'travelling' alone is not a full verb here.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition means 'together with' here: 'with' or 'by'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence about not being able to read everything.
options: I could not read every word, so some of it stays a mystery.
I could not read every words, so some of it stays a mystery.
I could not to read every word, so some of it stays a mystery.
I couldn't read each words, so some of it stays a mystery.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every word' (singular noun after 'every') and 'could not read' are correct. 'Every words' and 'each words' use a plural after a singular determiner; 'could not to read' adds a wrong 'to'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'every', is the noun singular or plural?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best closing sentence about displaying the map.
options: I keep the map in a frame now, recipe side outside.
I keep the map in a frame now, recipe side out.
I keep the map on a frame now, recipe side out.
I am keeping the map in a frame now, recipe side out always.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In a frame' and 'recipe side out' are natural. 'Side outside' is redundant; 'on a frame' uses the wrong preposition; the last adds an awkward 'always' with the continuous.
mcQuestions.hint: Do we put something 'in' or 'on' a frame?

criticalThinkingQuestion: What ordinary object in your home might tell a stranger something true about a person they never met? Why do small everyday things sometimes hold more history than important ones?
